lines: Find a comfortable position. Whatever feels safest right now. If you need a blanket around you, get one. If you need to hold something — a pillow, a piece of clothing — that's fine. Let your body have what it needs.
Close your eyes if that's comfortable. If closing your eyes feels too vulnerable right now, just soften your gaze toward the floor. Either is fine.
Take a breath. [long pause] It doesn't have to be deep. [long pause] It just has to be yours.

You're here because something hurts. [long pause] Maybe it's fresh — still raw, still sharp, still unbelievable. [long pause] Or maybe it's old — a dull ache you carry that flares up when you least expect it. [long pause] Or maybe it's anticipatory — a loss you see coming that you can't prevent.
Whatever it is... [long pause] [long pause] you don't have to explain it right now. [long pause] You don't have to make sense of it. [long pause] You just have to be here with it.

I want to start with a simple truth: grief is not a problem to solve. [long pause] It is not a stage to get through, a phase to complete, a feeling to manage until it goes away. [long pause] Grief is the natural response of a heart that loved something — and that something is gone, or going, or changed beyond recognition.
If you are grieving, it is because you loved. [long pause] And that is not weakness. [long pause] That is the most human thing there is.
The shortest verse in the Bible is two words. [long pause] "Jesus wept." John eleven, thirty-five.

His friend Lazarus had died. [long pause] And even though Jesus knew what he was about to do — even though resurrection was minutes away — he wept. [long pause] He stood at the tomb of someone he loved and he cried.
God in human skin... [long pause] [long pause] grieved.
Whatever permission you need to feel what you feel... [long pause] [long pause] that verse is it. [long pause] If the Son of God wept at a grave, you are allowed to weep at yours.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] let's find where grief lives in your body. [long pause] This is important. [long pause] Grief is not just an emotion. [long pause] It's a physical experience. [long pause] It has weight and texture and location.

Scan slowly from the top of your head... [long pause] [long pause] down through your face... [long pause] [long pause] your throat... [long pause] [long pause] your chest... [long pause] [long pause] your stomach... [long pause] [long pause] your legs. [long pause] Where is it? [long pause] Where does the grief sit?

For some people it's a heaviness in the chest, like something pressing down. [long pause] For others it's a hollowness in the belly — an emptiness that aches. [long pause] Sometimes it's a tightness in the throat, the place where words get stuck. [long pause] Sometimes it's exhaustion — a bone-deep tiredness that sleep doesn't fix.
Find yours. [long pause] You don't have to name it perfectly. [long pause] Just locate it. [long pause] Put your attention there like a gentle hand.

Stay with it. [long pause] I know the instinct is to move away. [long pause] To think about something else. [long pause] To be strong. [long pause] But right now, strength is this: staying present with what is true.
Breathe toward that place in your body. [long pause] Not to make it better. [long pause] Just to keep it company.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] I want you to name what you're grieving. [long pause] Not out loud if you don't want to. [long pause] Just inwardly. [long pause] It might be a person. [long pause] It might be a relationship. [long pause] It might be your health, or a dream, or a version of your life that will never happen now. [long pause] It might be the loss of who you used to be. [long pause] It might be many things layered on top of each other.
Name what comes. [long pause] One thing at a time.
If anger comes with the naming — let it come. [long pause] Grief and anger are not opposites. [long pause] They are neighbors. [long pause] Sometimes they live in the same room.

If anger at God comes... [long pause] [long pause] let that be there too. [long pause] The psalms are full of people shouting at God. [long pause] "How long, O Lord? [long pause] Will you forget me forever?" That is not a failure of faith. [long pause] That is faith being honest. [long pause] God is not threatened by your anger. [long pause] He'd rather have your honesty than your performance.
Psalm thirty-four, verse eighteen: "The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit."

Close to the brokenhearted. [long pause] Not close to the people who have it together. [long pause] Not close to the ones who've moved on. [long pause] Close to the brokenhearted. [long pause] If your heart is broken... [long pause] [long pause] you are exactly where God draws near.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] let me pray. [long pause] And this is a prayer of lament. [long pause] It does not end with resolution. [long pause] It does not tie a bow on the pain. [long pause] It is honest. [long pause] And that is enough.

God... [long pause] [long pause] we are here. [long pause] And we are hurting. [long pause] You know what we've lost — or what we're losing. [long pause] You know the name we just spoke inwardly, the face, the dream, the life we thought we'd have.

We don't understand it. [long pause] We may never understand it. [long pause] And right now, we are not asking for understanding. [long pause] We are asking for your presence. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] be here. [long pause] In the room with us. [long pause] In the ache with us. [long pause] In the anger and the silence and the two a.m. [long pause] moments when the grief comes back uninvited.

You are close to the brokenhearted. [long pause] Be close to us now. [long pause] Not with answers. [long pause] Not with explanations. [long pause] Not with timelines for healing. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] with yourself.
We hold this grief up to you. [long pause] Not to hand it off — because it's ours, and it matters. [long pause] But to let you see it. [long pause] To let you hold it with us.
Jesus... [long pause] [long pause] you wept. [long pause] So we weep. [long pause] And we trust that our tears are not wasted on you.

Stay as long as you need. [long pause] There is no rush to feel better. [long pause] There is no right way to do this. [long pause] If tears come — let them. [long pause] If numbness comes — that's okay too. [long pause] If nothing comes — that is also grief.

When you're ready... [long pause] [long pause] begin with your body. [long pause] Feel your hands. [long pause] Move your fingers slowly. [long pause] Feel your feet. [long pause] Take a breath that goes all the way to the bottom of your lungs.
You are still here. [long pause] You carried something heavy today, and you stayed present with it. [long pause] That is not small. [long pause] That is courage.

And the Lord is close to you. [long pause] Right now. [long pause] Not because you did this meditation correctly. [long pause] Because your heart is broken. [long pause] And that is exactly where he meets you.
